attributes.description: Full hardware telemetry during gaming sessions: GPU temperature, power, clocks, CPU utilisation and thermals, memory pressure, storage throughput, and power profile. Essential for hardware reviewers, OEM engineers, and thermal analysis.

query.esql: FROM metrics-gamepulse.session-default | WHERE gamepulse.hardware.gpu.model IS NOT NULL | STATS `GPU` = MAX(gamepulse.hardware.gpu.model)
FROM metrics-gamepulse.session-default | WHERE gamepulse.hardware.gpu.model IS NOT NULL | STATS `GPU` = MAX(gamepulse.hardware.gpu.model)
FROM metrics-gamepulse.session-default | WHERE gamepulse.hardware.cpu.model IS NOT NULL | STATS `CPU` = MAX(gamepulse.hardware.cpu.model)
FROM metrics-gamepulse.session-default | WHERE gamepulse.hardware.cpu.model IS NOT NULL | STATS `CPU` = MAX(gamepulse.hardware.cpu.model)
FROM metrics-gamepulse.session-default | WHERE gamepulse.hardware.ram.total_mb IS NOT NULL | STATS `RAM GB` = ROUND(MAX(gamepulse.hardware.ram.total_mb) / 1024.0, 0)
FROM metrics-gamepulse.session-default | WHERE gamepulse.hardware.ram.total_mb IS NOT NULL | STATS `RAM GB` = ROUND(MAX(gamepulse.hardware.ram.total_mb) / 1024.0, 0)
FROM metrics-gamepulse.session-default | WHERE gamepulse.hardware.gpu.vram_mb IS NOT NULL | STATS `VRAM GB` = ROUND(MAX(gamepulse.hardware.gpu.vram_mb) / 1024.0, 1)
FROM metrics-gamepulse.session-default | WHERE gamepulse.hardware.gpu.vram_mb IS NOT NULL | STATS `VRAM GB` = ROUND(MAX(gamepulse.hardware.gpu.vram_mb) / 1024.0, 1)

embeddableConfig.title: GPU Temperature Over Time — Most AMD/NVIDIA GPUs throttle above 100°C junction / 110°C hotspot. Watch for gradual temperature rise indicating throttling across a long session.
GPU Power Draw Over Time — Sustained high draw near card TDP = power-limited; watch for throttling. Power spikes on Vulkan/DX12 async compute reflect burst workloads.
GPU Core Utilisation Over Time — 100% = GPU fully saturated. Sub-100% during gameplay indicates CPU bottleneck or driver overhead. Low + low FPS = likely CPU-bound.
GPU Core Clock Over Time — Clock below rated boost speed indicates thermal or power throttling. Sustained clock reduction simultaneous with high temperature = thermal throttle.
CPU Utilisation Over Time — Total CPU utilisation and per-core load. High total% with low GPU util = CPU bottleneck. Uneven per-core load suggests the game is not using all available cores.
CPU Temperature + Clock Over Time — Watch for temperature-induced frequency throttling (PROCHOT). Clock drop simultaneous with temp rise = thermal throttle.
System Memory Usage Over Time — Game RSS = resident set size (physical memory used by the game process). High page cache hit rate is desirable; major page faults indicate RAM pressure.
Swap + Page Cache Over Time — Swap usage during gaming = system is RAM-constrained. Evicting page cache under memory pressure causes asset re-load stutters.

Storage Throughput Over Time — High read throughput during gameplay indicates asset streaming or shader cache loading. NVMe drives should sustain > 1 GB/s; HDD-class storage may bottleneck texture streaming.
Power Profile Over Time — Battery drain rate, TDP, and power profile. Battery devices: sustained gaming drains ~10–25 W on handheld hardware.
Hardware Configurations Seen — grouped by GPU model

attributes.title: GamePulse — Hardware Environment
